title: Blue Origin gets national security launch task order hours before New Glenn explosion
summary: Blue Origin received a national security launch task order from U.S. Space Force and National Reconnaissance Office just hours before the explosion of its New Glenn rocket. The agencies reaffirmed their commitment to Blue Origin as a partner despite the incident.

title: Space Force awards SpaceX $4.16 billion to build satellite network for airborne target tracking
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded SpaceX a $4.16 billion contract to develop a low-orbit satellite network for tracking airborne threats like aircraft and cruise missiles. The constellation will enhance real-time monitoring of potential military targets.

title: Space Force’s commercial gatekeeper offers a playbook for startups seeking defense business
summary: Space Force's commercial gatekeeper outlines key strategies for startups aiming to work with the defense sector, emphasizing transparency, patience, and a clear military value proposition alongside technical innovation.

title: Bellatrix and TelePIX plan 2028 air-breathing VLEO imaging demonstration
summary: TelePIX and Bellatrix Aerospace are collaborating on a 2028 demonstration mission for air-breathing very low Earth orbit (VLEO) imaging. The mission aims to showcase geospatial capabilities using innovative propulsion technology.

title: Spatial data has become a weapon of war in the US-Iran war
summary: Spatial data derived from satellites is being used as a strategic weapon in the US-Iran conflict, marking a shift toward geospatial warfare. The distinction between civilian and military satellite data is becoming increasingly blurred.

title: China’s latest batch of new and reusable rockets are close to launch
summary: China is preparing to launch several new and reusable rockets, including both state-led and commercial efforts, with debuts and recovery attempts expected in the coming months.

title: New Glenn rocket explodes on Cape Canaveral pad
summary: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded during a hotfire test at Cape Canaveral's launch pad on May 28, resulting in the destruction of the rocket and significant pad damage.

title: Space Force plans nationwide network of ‘resilient operations centers’
summary: The U.S. Space Force is planning to establish a nationwide network of resilient operations centers to ensure military space missions can continue during wartime attacks. These facilities aim to enhance the resilience and survivability of critical space operations.

title: Observable Space raises $90 million and wins Space Force contract for optical systems
summary: Observable Space raised $90 million and secured a U.S. Space Force contract to develop optical systems for laser communications and space domain awareness.

title: Setting up a permanent lunar presence needs investment in biology
summary: Establishing a permanent human presence on the Moon requires significant investment in biological research and life support systems. The Apollo 17 mission in 1972 marked the last time humans walked on the lunar surface.

title: 'For All Mankind' star Wrenn Schmidt on her character's journey from NASA's mission control to the slammer (interview)
summary: Wrenn Schmidt discusses her character's transformation from NASA mission control to prison in the show 'For All Mankind'. The interview highlights her character's exposure to new experiences while incarcerated.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 212 — Kabooms, Starship, and a Moon Base
summary: Episode 212 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast discusses the loss of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket, along with other topics like Starship and a potential Moon base.

title: Watch the smallest full moon of 2026 tonight in this rare 'Blue Moon' livestream
summary: The Virtual Telescope Project will livestream the smallest full moon of 2026, known as a 'Blue Moon,' starting at 9:30 p.m. EDT (0130 GMT) from Italy.

title: From 'Tetris' to 'StarCraft 2' — 5 times astronauts took video games into space
summary: Astronauts have taken video games like 'Tetris' and 'StarCraft 2' into space for entertainment. This highlights the unique recreational activities enjoyed by astronauts during their missions.

title: The Blue Moon rises tonight: Where and when to see the second full moon of May
summary: The second full moon of May, known as the Blue Moon, will be visible tonight. This astronomical event occurs when two full moons appear in a single calendar month.

title: 'Star City': Release date & how to watch Apple TV's superb Soviet space series
summary: 'Star City' is a new Apple TV spinoff series set in the Soviet space program, offering a behind-the-scenes look at the Cold War-era space race. The series is a spin-off of the popular alternate-history show 'For All Mankind'.

title: China's Shenzhou 21 astronauts return to Earth after being briefly 'stranded', wrapping up record-breaking mission (video)
summary: China's Shenzhou 21 astronauts safely returned to Earth after completing a record-breaking mission, which included a brief 'stranding' incident. The mission marked a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Rare Blue Moon hides the red light of an ancient star on May 31: Will you be able to see it?
summary: A rare Blue Moon will coincide with the red star Antares on May 31, creating a visually striking celestial event. This marks the second full moon of May, offering a unique astronomical sight.

title: NASA Awards Contract for Johnson Space Center Infrastructure
summary: NASA has awarded a contract to seven companies for infrastructure upgrades at Johnson Space Center in Houston, with a potential value of up to $300 million. The improvements include mission-support facilities, utilities, and equipment, with funds to be obligated by September 30, 2026.

title: NASA Hosts SpaceX Crew-11 Astronauts for Public Event at Headquarters
summary: NASA is hosting a public event for three astronauts from the SpaceX Crew-11 mission at its headquarters in Washington, D.C. The event will take place on June 1 at the Webb Auditorium in the Mary W. Jackson building.

title: NASA’s Roman Space Telescope Primary Mirror Gets Last Look
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope's primary mirror has passed its final inspection. The 7.9-foot mirror will enable the telescope to capture wide-field images of the universe.

title: Hubble Spies Faint Irregular Galaxy
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the dwarf irregular galaxy ESO 490-017, located 23 million light-years away in the constellation Canis Major. The galaxy's low surface brightness and faint appearance are highlighted against brighter foreground stars.

title: Hubble Captures M88 on Journey to Center of Virgo Cluster
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the spiral galaxy Messier 88 (M88), located approximately 63 million light-years away. The galaxy is on a journey toward the center of the Virgo Cluster.

title: Painting the Growing Season in the Maize Triangle
summary: Radar data from an agricultural area in South Africa reveals crop types and their changes during the Southern Hemisphere's growing season, visualized in a vivid color palette.

title: NASA’s X-59 Prepares for First Supersonic Flight
summary: NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic research aircraft is gearing up for its first supersonic flight as part of a new block of test missions. The aircraft will also conduct other critical mission objectives during these upcoming flights.

title: I Am Artemis: Daniel Stubbs
summary: NASA aerospace engineer Daniel Stubbs discusses potential challenges for Artemis human landing systems due to lunar dust during landing. The issue is compared to driving through a cloud of dust that obscures visibility.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4900-4907: Pasadena, We Have a Drill Sample!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover successfully drilled a sample at 'Campo Marte' on Mars, as confirmed by downlink data. The operation was part of ongoing scientific exploration by the Jet Propulsion Laboratory team.
company: Jet Propulsion Laboratory, California Institute of Technology

title: NASA Uses Mineralogical Marker to Understand Ancient Martian Climate
summary: Scientists used NASA’s Curiosity Rover to analyze 20 Martian samples, finding that hematite crystallite size variations could reveal insights about Mars' ancient climate.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a catastrophic explosion during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36. The anomaly occurred during a planned hot-fire test of the vehicle.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, utilizing rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative involves rebranding existing Commercial Lunar projects.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted the debut flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo mission among the highlights.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance activities throughout April. The ongoing operations highlight the station's role in supporting research and logistics in low Earth orbit.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to the Tiangong space station, which has been permanently occupied since June 5, 2022. This marks another mission to maintain China's ongoing presence in low Earth orbit.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, from Boca Chica, Texas, demonstrating significant improvements over previous flights. The mission marked a major milestone for SpaceX's next-generation rocket system.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's Starship Flight 12, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C flight.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, highlighting its ongoing improvements. It is presented as a key advancement in the company's launch vehicle technology.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping ESA test and select materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space for its missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh perspectives.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) to enhance training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A baking experiment was conducted to test the heat resistance of parachutes for space missions. The process involved subjecting parachute materials to high temperatures to simulate re-entry conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination on Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling improved handling of objects in orbit. This innovation could enhance satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn Explodes on the Pad
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a catastrophic failure during its launch attempt, resulting in an explosion on the pad. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's ambitions in the heavy-lift launch market.

title: Europe Celebrates its Sovereignty Progress at SmallSat
summary: Europe highlighted its progress in achieving orbital sovereignty at the SmallSat Europe event. The gathering emphasized the continent's advancements in space independence.

title: Observable Space Raises $90M Series A
summary: Observable Space, a space-tech startup, has secured $90 million in Series A funding to expand its production of laser and optical hardware and grow internationally. The company aims to scale its operations beyond the US with the new capital.

title: Space Markets Emerges From Stealth With Coinbase Ventures Investment
summary: Space Markets has emerged from stealth mode with a new investment from Coinbase Ventures. The company aims to create futures markets for space-related commodities and resources.

title: Exclusive: Stellar Alpina Raises $4.5M to Build Rotating Detonation Engines
summary: Stellar Alpina, a European propulsion startup, has raised $4.5 million to develop rotating detonation engines, a novel technology aimed at advancing spacecraft propulsion. This funding will support the company's efforts to bring this innovative thrust technology to market.

title: Aitech Upgrades its Space Supercomputer
summary: Aitech has upgraded its space supercomputer by integrating NVIDIA's IGX Thor platform into the S-A2300 COTS AI Supercomputer, enhancing in-orbit data processing capabilities. This upgrade significantly expands customers' ability to handle data-intensive tasks in space.

title: NASA Awards LTV, Lunar Transport Contracts
summary: NASA has awarded contracts for Lunar Terrain Vehicles (LTV) and lunar transport services, marking a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon. The announcement was made during a press conference at NASA Headquarters.

title: Op-ed: Commercial Demand in LEO is Lacking. The Government Can Help.
summary: A recent op-ed argues that the most promising commercial opportunity in Low Earth Orbit (LEO)—in-space manufacturing—currently lacks sufficient demand to sustain itself, and calls for government support to drive growth.

title: Optical Links: When to Incorporate Them? When Not To?
summary: The article discusses the cost-effectiveness of optical links for satellite applications, noting that they often cost hundreds of thousands of dollars and may not be worth the investment. It explores scenarios where optical links are beneficial versus when they are not.

title: Satellogic Lands $18M+ Defense-Monitoring Contract
summary: Satellogic secured a defense-monitoring contract worth over $18 million, marking its first major agreement following a strategic pivot to persistent-monitoring capabilities.

title: SpaceX Gets Nearly $4.2 Billion Nod From Space Force For Initial SB-AMTI
summary: SpaceX received a nearly $4.2 billion contract from the U.S. Space Force for the initial Space Based Advanced Missile Threat Identification (SB-AMTI) program. This follows a recent $2.3 billion award for the Space Data Network Backbone.

title: Sateliot Wins New Telenor IoT Deal
summary: Sateliot, a Spanish satellite company, has secured a new IoT partnership with Telenor IoT, a division of the Nordic telco giant Telenor Group.

title: Viasat Full Year ‘26 Takeaways: Record Revenue, Equatys Moves Forward
summary: Viasat reported record revenue and awards in 2026 but missed stabilization goals in maritime and fixed broadband. The company's Equatys initiative continued to progress.

title: Blue Origin’s Launch Pad Explosion Could Impact Timelines for Commercial Customers, NASA Missions
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a launch pad explosion at Cape Canaveral, potentially delaying commercial and NASA missions. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's spaceflight timelines.

title: EU Targets User Terminal Tech Advancements Ahead of IRIS²
summary: The European Union has issued a tender for innovative and cost-effective user terminal technologies for the IRIS² satellite constellation. This initiative aims to advance user terminal tech ahead of the constellation's deployment.

title: BlackSky Advances Non-Earth Imaging Business With 7-Figure Renewal
summary: BlackSky Technology secured a significant contract renewal worth seven figures for non-Earth imagery services, where its satellites capture images of objects in orbit. This advancement highlights the growing demand for space-based observation in orbital monitoring.

title: UK’s O2 Satellite Expands D2D Service to iPhones
summary: UK’s O2 Satellite has expanded its direct-to-device (D2D) service to support iPhones. The service leverages SpaceX’s Starlink Mobile constellation, which launched earlier this year.

title: Swissto12 Adds to Leadership Team to Target Global Growth
summary: Swissto12 is expanding its leadership team with new roles including a chief commercial officer (CCO) and executives focused on government affairs and partnerships to support global growth. The company's CTO is also mentioned in the context of this expansion.

title: Orbital Data Centers Must Tackle Chip Lifespan, Launch Availability, and Cybersecurity Challenges
summary: Orbital data centers face challenges like chip lifespan, launch availability, and cybersecurity. The discussion is highlighted in the context of SpaceX's initial IPO documents.

title: Will the European Launch Market Finally Ignite?
summary: The European space industry is seeking more efficient and reliable access to space, a topic that dominated discussions at a recent launch panel in Amsterdam. The industry is questioning when it will achieve this goal.
